prompts.question:
1. Use & Reduction

a. Have you tried to cut back on your alcohol or drug (both prescribed and non-prescribed) use? What challenges did you face?

b. How much were you using before and after trying to reduce?

2. Current Use

a. How often do you drink alcohol? How much do you drink?

b. How often do you use drugs (both prescribed and not prescribed)? How much do you use?

c. How do you consume marijuana? Do you have a medical license for marijuana? How much do you use?

3. Impact on Life

a. Has your substance use ever affected your relationships, work, or responsibilities? Have others told you that your use of substances have impacted them?

b. Is it impacting your well-being or health? How has your use impacted your life?

4. Treatment & Legal Issues

a. Have you undergone detox or inpatient treatment? When did you get treatment? Where was it at?

b. Do you have any legal issues related to substance use?

1. Access & Communication

a. Do you know where to go for healthcare when needed?

b. How do you contact your PCP, and what information do you share with your provider and how do you share it?

c. When do you reach out to a specialist versus your PCP or the ED?

d. Do you have a reliable phone number and website access for lab results and appointments?

2. Medications & Equipment

a. Can you access, afford, and refill your medications?

b. Do you have transportation to pick them up or can get them delivered to you?

c. Can you get medical equipment like CPAP machines, walkers, or safety bars if you need them?

3. Home Safety & Care

a. Is your home safe and easy to get into?

b. Do you receive or have you received home care services (PT, OT, nursing)? What type? When did you receive it?

i. If discontinued, was it your choice or not? Why or why not?

1. Access & Reliability

a. How do you usually get around?

b. Do you have trouble getting to medical appointments?

2. Public & Medical Transport

a. Do you have an active or discounted bus pass?

b. Are you comfortable using public transit?

c. Do you know how to access medical transportation, including Medicaid services?

3. Special Needs & Assistance

a. Do you require special accommodations (wheelchair, walker, hearing assistance)?

b. Do you use transportation apps? Which ones? Are they easy?

c. Do you rely on friends, family, or neighbors for rides?

a. Do you understand your health conditions and the information from your providers?

b. Do you know your medications and when to take them? Do you feel you are taking the right medications?

c. Do you know what to do if your condition worsens?

d. Do you know when your health is an emergency or can wait until the clinic can see you?

a. Have you been feeling down, anxious, or overwhelmed?

b. Have you had thoughts of harming yourself or others?

c. How long have you felt this way, and what have you tried to improve your mood?

d. What treatments—medical or nonmedical—have you used? Are you seeing a mental health provider?

e. Have you noticed changes in sleep, energy, activity, appetite, or weight?

f. Have others noticed a change in you?

a. Can your income cover your needs like housing and utilities?

b. Do you struggle to afford housing? Have you applied for assistance or housing programs?

c. Do you have access to emergency shelter options if you need it?

d. Do you have trouble paying for utilities, like power, water, or internet?

e. Have you applied for financial assistance for housing or utilities?

f. If you own a home, can you afford property taxes?

a. Have you ever worried about where your next meal will come from?

b. Do you have access to food banks or food assistance programs?

c. Are you enrolled in food delivery programs like Meals on Wheels or Project Angelheart?

d. Are others in your household also facing food insecurity? Who?

a. Is stress interfering with your daily life?

b. Have recent life changes triggered new symptoms or increased stress?

c. Has stress affected your sleep, mood, or overall well-being?

d. What are your biggest sources of stress?

e. Have you sought support from mental health professionals, spiritual leaders, or loved ones?

a. Are you currently employed? If not, are you seeking work?

b. Can you work full-time or part-time?

c. Does your job meet your financial and personal needs?

d. What do you enjoy about your work? What could improve?
